disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Recent open abdominal (gallbladder) operation 4 days ago with a surgical drain left in the upper right abdomen
trigger_question_examples: What operation did you have and when?
Why do you have a drain in?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I had my gallbladder removed with an open operation 4 days ago and they left a drain tube in the upper right of my belly.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: One hour ago the drain tubing was snagged and mechanically pulled partway out; sharp tearing pain since, with the securing stitch broken
trigger_question_examples: What happened just before the pain started?
Has the tube moved or come out at all?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: About an hour ago I caught the tube on a chair and it pulled out about halfway — the little stitch snapped and it has hurt sharply ever since.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Drain output had reduced to minimal or nil over the 24 hours before the event
trigger_question_examples: How much has the drain been draining lately?
Was the drain still collecting much fluid before this happened?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It had almost stopped draining over the last day — hardly anything was coming out.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Bleeding at the site is minor and localized to the skin edge — not soaking through
trigger_question_examples: Is there any bleeding, and how much?
Is blood soaking through any dressing or your clothes?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: There's just a little blood right at the skin — it's not pouring out or soaking anything.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No fever, shivering, spreading redness or pus around the site before the event
trigger_question_examples: Have you had any fevers or shivers?
Was the skin around the drain red, hot, or oozing pus before today?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No fevers or shivers, and the skin around it wasn't red or leaking pus before this happened.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No visible bulge or protruding tissue at the site; abdomen is not rigid or swollen; the patient can still describe localized rather than generalized pain
trigger_question_examples: Is anything bulging or poking out of the wound?
Is the pain just at the tube, or has it spread across your whole belly?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Nothing is bulging or poking out that I can see, and the pain is right at the tube, not all over my belly.

trigger_question_examples: Is anyone with you right now?
Do you have someone who can help or drive you?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, I live alone and I'm on my own here at the moment.
